headline: Cameron Highlands Lost 2,300 Hectares to Illegal Farms
context: A 2025 JUPEM aerial survey found 2,300 hectares of illegally cleared agricultural land in Cameron Highlands, much of it on steep slopes exceeding 25 degrees.

cards.big: Pahang state issued 340 stop-work orders in Cameron Highlands since 2020.
cards.sub: Follow-up inspections found 89% of sites continued operating. Zero prosecutions resulted.
NIDM recorded 47 landslides in Cameron Highlands in 2024 alone — 12 damaged homes and roads.

cards.big: Illegal farms employ an estimated 15,000 undocumented workers.
cards.sub: Enforcement is complicated by immigration status — raiding farms triggers humanitarian concerns.

cards.big: Agricultural output from Cameron Highlands is worth RM2.8B annually.
cards.sub: An estimated 40% originates from unlicensed operations on illegally cleared land.

cards.big: 340 stop-work orders with zero prosecutions is not enforcement. It is paperwork.
Cameron Highlands needs a land audit that distinguishes legal from illegal operations, followed by enforcement with actual consequences. Neither has happened.
